description: Replace plain Error throws with structured ToolError factory methods in starbucks tool handlers, so the AI agent receives properly categorized errors.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/starbucks && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Replace throw new Error() with ToolError factory methods in starbucks tools
userStories.description: In plugins/starbucks/src/tools/add-product-to-cart.ts and update-product-quantity.ts, multiple plain Error throws bypass the ToolError dispatch chain. Replace each with the appropriate ToolError factory method based on the error semantics: product not found → ToolError.notFound, size not found → ToolError.validation, no sizes available → ToolError.notFound, Redux store unavailable → ToolError.internal.
acceptanceCriteria: No plain `new Error(...)` throws remain in add-product-to-cart.ts or update-product-quantity.ts
Product not found errors use ToolError.notFound()
Size not found errors use ToolError.validation() (caller provided an invalid size)
Redux store unavailable errors use ToolError.internal()
ToolError is imported from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk' in both files
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/starbucks/src/tools/add-product-to-cart.ts (lines 69, 78, 83, 108)
- plugins/starbucks/src/tools/update-product-quantity.ts (lines 36, 42)

Context:
add-product-to-cart.ts:
  Line 69: throw new Error(`Product ${params.product_number}/${params.form} not found`)
    → throw ToolError.notFound(`Product ${params.product_number}/${params.form} not found`)
  Line 78: throw new Error(`Size "${params.size}" not found. Available: ${sizes.map(s => s.sizeCode).join(', ')}`)
    → throw ToolError.validation(`Size "${params.size}" not found. Available: ${sizes.map(s => s.sizeCode).join(', ')}`)
  Line 83: throw new Error('No sizes available for this product')
    → throw ToolError.notFound('No sizes available for this product')
  Line 108: throw new Error('Redux store not available')
    → throw ToolError.internal('Redux store not available')

Read update-product-quantity.ts lines 36 and 42 to find the throw new Error() calls and apply the appropriate ToolError type based on the error semantics.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the error messages (keep them as is)
- Make sure ToolError is imported — add it to the import from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk' if not already present
- Do NOT change any other logic in these files
